rag_rules: Fix AGREE diagnostics by resolving type errors and invalid references in implementation-level contracts.
Ensure that implementation-level guarantees only reference valid outputs or assignments within the component implementation scope.
generic_rules: When AGREE reports type mismatches, align helper declarations with the correct expression types (bool, int, real).
If a guarantee or assumption references an invalid identifier, remove or rewrite the clause to use only visible AADL features.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the AADL model during repair.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE expressions must resolve to valid types; use only declared helpers or visible component features.
Implementation-level AGREE clauses should be placed in the component implementation annex, not the type annex.
anti_patterns: Do not add new identifiers or types to AGREE contracts that are not already present in the AADL model.
Do not attempt to fix unresolved references by inventing new declarations; instead, rewrite or remove the clause.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE annex placement in component types vs implementations
Model Analyst agent's role in extracting AADL structure
